title: codex-tool-calls
description: Tool calls Codex emits in its final delegate response. The codex-companion runtime parses one fenced ```json codex-tool-calls block, validates against this schema, and dispatches each call locally (file edits, inbox writes, bash). Codex never invokes Claude Code primitives directly — this schema is the bridge contract. Claude or Codex updates change ONLY this file.

TeamSend.description: Append a message to a teammate's inbox file. Effect = SendMessage tool.

EditFile.description: Find old_string in path and replace with new_string (single occurrence). Effect = Edit tool.

WriteFile.description: Create or overwrite a file at path with content. Effect = Write tool.

RunBash.description: Run a shell command synchronously. Output (stdout + stderr) is included in the dispatch report. Effect = Bash tool.

AskLead.description: Convenience wrapper around team_send that formats a decision request to team-lead and signals NEEDS_FOLLOW_UP. The companion does NOT halt the codex thread; codex must still emit STATUS: NEEDS_FOLLOW_UP at the end of the response for the bridge skill / agent to recognize the pause point.

PushNotification.description: Surface a notification to the user — typically when a long-running task hits a milestone they'd want to know about (long codex run finished, error needs attention). Companion writes to stderr + falls back to team_send to team-lead so the message reaches the user even if Claude Code's PushNotification primitive is unreachable. Use sparingly: most events should go via team_send instead. Effect ≈ Claude Code's PushNotification tool.

TodoWrite.description: Ask team-lead to track a structured todo list. Companion forwards as a formatted team_send (team-lead can then call its own TodoWrite tool to persist). Useful when codex wants to plan visible multi-step work. Effect ≈ Claude Code's TodoWrite tool, mediated by team-lead.
